reason: Both answers correctly diagnose the Goodhart-style mean-hiding-distribution problem and converge on similar fixes: SLO/error-budget burn, total impact-minutes, distribution instead of mean, recurrence rate, and pager/toil metrics for engineers. Both are concise and hit all four key points. B edges ahead slightly by also separating MTTD from MTTR (detection lag not counted), proposing a concrete severity-weighted single number, and giving a sharper closing heuristic. The difference is marginal but B covers slightly more decision-relevant ground without padding.
